title: Create full-screen canvas shell
summary: Wire up a full-viewport <canvas> with resize tracking, dark background, and no scrollbars. The visual modules (scene.ts, resize.ts, renderLoop.ts) and CSS already exist from prior work; the plan focuses on verifying correctness, filling any gaps, and adding acceptance-criteria tests.

existingFiles: src/visual/scene.ts — creates canvas, 2D context, fills black
src/visual/resize.ts — window resize listener, updates canvas dimensions
src/main.ts — imports visual modules, appends canvas to #app
missingPieces: Canvas may not account for devicePixelRatio scaling
Resize handler may not handle DPR changes

dependencies.threeJs: NOT installed — not needed for US-002 (2D placeholder is sufficient)

steps.detail: Verify the existing CSS covers all acceptance criteria: margin/padding reset on *, html, body; overflow:hidden; width/height 100vw/100vh; background:#000. Add box-sizing:border-box if missing. Ensure the #app container and canvas itself also have no margin/padding and display:block to prevent inline-element whitespace gaps.

Ensure initScene() creates a <canvas>, sizes it to window.innerWidth × window.innerHeight, appends it to the container, and returns the canvas + context. Canvas element should have display:block and no default border. Consider devicePixelRatio for sharp rendering: set canvas.width/height to logical×DPR while keeping CSS size at 100%. Return canvas reference so resize handler can update it.

Ensure the resize handler updates both canvas.width/height (backing store) and canvas.style.width/height (CSS) on window resize. Must use the same DPR logic as scene.ts. Should debounce or use requestAnimationFrame to avoid layout thrash on rapid resizing.

Confirm main.ts imports style.css, calls initScene(container), attachResizeHandler(canvas), and startLoop(ctx). Ensure the #app container is found before proceeding. No changes expected unless earlier modules changed their exports.

Create tests/us-002.test.ts covering all four acceptance criteria: (1) canvas fills viewport — canvas.width >= window.innerWidth and canvas.height >= window.innerHeight; (2) resize updates render size — simulate window resize event, assert canvas dimensions update; (3) no scrollbars — document.documentElement.scrollHeight <= window.innerHeight and same for width, or overflow:hidden is set; (4) dark background — computed background-color of body is #000 or rgb(0,0,0). Use jsdom environment.
Execute npm run test and npm run build. Fix any failures. Verify no console errors by checking test output.

acceptanceCriteriaMapping.Canvas fills the viewport: Steps 1-2: CSS sets 100vw/100vh, scene.ts sizes canvas to window dimensions
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.Viewport resize updates render size: Step 3: resize.ts listens for window resize and updates canvas
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.No scrollbars appear: Step 1: overflow:hidden on html/body, box-sizing:border-box, canvas display:block

testStrategy: jsdom-based unit tests asserting DOM state after module initialization. Build verification via tsc && vite build. Manual smoke test: dev server shows black full-screen canvas with no scrollbars, resizing browser window updates canvas.
risks: jsdom does not fully support canvas 2D context — tests may need to mock getContext or use a canvas polyfill
devicePixelRatio is not available in jsdom — DPR-aware sizing tests need to stub window.devicePixelRatio
outOfScope: Any visual content beyond a black placeholder canvas
